title: micranthes stellaris subsp. hispidula
intro: Micranthes stellaris subsp. hispidula, also known as the alpine rock jasmine or mountain starwort, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the saxifrage family and is native to the high-altitude regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of sore throats, colds, and other respiratory issues. The health benefits of Micranthes stellaris subsp. hispidula include its ability to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and boost the immune system, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for a convenient and accessible way to harness its medicinal properties.
